content:
# Revelation Chapter 17 -- Disagreement Log
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **Book:** Revelation | **Chapter:** 17

**Verse 1:**
- EMET: "the judgment of the great harlot, the [one] sitting upon many waters"
- LOGOS: "the judgment of the great harlot who sits upon many waters"
- PNEUMA: "the sentencing of the great prostitute who sits enthroned over many waters"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA adopts "the judgment of the great harlot who sits upon many waters." The Greek *krima* is best rendered "judgment" (EMET/LOGOS) rather than "sentencing" (PNEUMA). "Harlot" is preferred over "prostitute" for consistency with Sinaiticus register. PNEUMA's "enthroned" is interpretive. LOGOS's relative clause structure is adopted.

**Verse 2:**
- EMET: "the [ones] dwelling upon the earth were made drunk from the wine of the fornication of her"
- LOGOS: "those who dwell upon the earth were made drunk with the wine of her fornication"
- PNEUMA: "the inhabitants of the earth have been made drunk on the wine of her debauchery"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS. "Fornication" retained over PNEUMA's "debauchery" as closer to *porneia*.

**Verse 4:**
- EMET: "having been gilded with gold"
- LOGOS: "adorned with gold"
- PNEUMA: "glittering with gold, precious stones"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA adopts "adorned" (LOGOS). PNEUMA pluralizes "stones" against Sinaiticus singular.

**Verse 5:**
- EMET: all-capitals, "THE MOTHER OF THE HARLOTS AND OF THE ABOMINATIONS OF THE EARTH"
- LOGOS: mixed case, "the Mother of the Harlots and of the Abominations of the Earth"
- PNEUMA: all-capitals, "MOTHER OF PROSTITUTES AND OF EVERY ABOMINATION ON EARTH"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA uses all-capitals with EMET/LOGOS wording: "THE MOTHER OF THE HARLOTS AND OF THE ABOMINATIONS OF THE EARTH."

**Verse 6:**
- EMET: "the holy [ones]... witnesses of Yeshua... great amazement"
- LOGOS: "the saints... witnesses of Jesus... great wonder"
- PNEUMA: "God's holy people... bore witness to Jesus... utterly astonished"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA uses "saints" (LOGOS), "Jesus" (LOGOS/PNEUMA), "great amazement" (EMET).

**Verse 7:**
- EMET: "the [one] carrying her"
- LOGOS: "the beast that carries her"
- PNEUMA: "the beast she rides"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS. PNEUMA reverses the grammatical subject.

**Verse 8:**
- EMET: "whose name" (singular) / "scroll of the life"
- LOGOS: "whose names" (plural) / "book of life"
- PNEUMA: "whose names" (plural) / "Book of Life"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA uses singular "name" (EMET, per Sinaiticus *onoma*) and "book of life" (LOGOS).

**Verse 9:**
- EMET: "seven mountains, where the woman sits upon them"
- LOGOS: "seven mountains upon which the woman sits"
- PNEUMA: "seven hills on which the woman sits enthroned"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS. "Mountains" over "hills" for *ore*; no "enthroned."

**Verse 13:**
- EMET: "one mind"
- LOGOS: "one purpose"
- PNEUMA: "united in purpose"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA uses "one purpose" (LOGOS) for *gnome*.

**Verse 14:**
- EMET: "conquer"
- LOGOS: "overcome"
- PNEUMA: "triumph over"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA uses "overcome" (LOGOS) for *nikesei*.

**Verse 15:**
- EMET: "which you saw"
- LOGOS: "that you saw"
- PNEUMA: "you saw" / "prostitute sits enthroned"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS. "Harlot" retained; no "enthroned."

**Verse 16:**
- EMET: hyper-literal word order preserving Greek construction
- LOGOS: "will make her desolate and naked, and will eat her flesh"
- PNEUMA: "will strip her bare, devour her flesh"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS for clarity while preserving Greek terms.

**Verse 17:**
- EMET: "the God gave... to do the purpose... the words of the God will be completed"
- LOGOS: "God put it into their hearts to do his purpose... words of God are fulfilled"
- PNEUMA: "God has put it into their hearts to carry out his purpose... God's words are fulfilled"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA blends: "carry out" from PNEUMA, "fulfilled" from LOGOS/PNEUMA.

-- SOPHIA
